title: Algorithms 2: Complexity and Graphs
description: Time and space complexity with Big-O notation, graphs as an abstract data structure, BFS/DFS traversal, and Dijkstra's shortest-path algorithm.
objectives: Explain time and space complexity, and use Big-O notation to describe how an algorithm's resource use grows with input size
Classify algorithms into complexity classes (O(1), O(log n), O(n), O(n log n), O(n^2), O(2^n)) and identify best-, worst- and average-case behaviour
Represent a graph using vertices and edges, distinguishing directed/undirected and weighted/unweighted graphs
Build and compare adjacency list and adjacency matrix representations of a graph
Trace breadth-first search (BFS) and depth-first search (DFS) traversals of a graph by hand
Trace Dijkstra's algorithm to find the shortest path between two vertices in a small weighted graph

lessons.title: Graph traversal: BFS, DFS and Dijkstra's algorithm
